perturbation.original_text: This Joint Research Agreement ("JRA") is effective as of May 25, 2005 by and between Nanolution, LLC ("Nanolution") and NaturalNano Inc. ("NaturalNano") (also referred to herein as "the Parties").
perturbation.changed_text: This Joint Research Agreement ("JRA") is effective as of May 25, 2005 by and between Nanolution, LLC ("Nanolution") and NaturalNano Inc. ("NaturalNano") (also referred to herein as "the Companies").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Parties' is replaced with 'Companies'. Later in the agreement, obligations are assigned to 'Parties', creating ambiguity whether 'Companies' and 'Parties' are the same, thereby creating uncertainty regarding the obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Joint Research Agreement ("JRA") is effective as of May 25, 2005 by and between Nanolution, LLC ("Nanolution") and NaturalNano Inc. ("NaturalNano") (also referred to herein as "the Companies"). The parties have agreed to conduct and support joint research in the field of drug delivery utilizing naturally occurring halloysite nanotechnologies ("the Project"). This JRA covers the exchange of ideas and information as well as the performance of experimental, developmental, and/or research work ("the Work") performed under the Project, and the ownership of confidential or proprietary information, including any intellectual property. The term of the Project shall be from May 25, 2005 and shall continue at least until the desired drug delivery technology has been proven commercially viable, or until mutually terminated by both parties. The Work performed as a result of the Project shall be subject to this JRA regardless of where the Work is performed or by whom.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the entities as 'Parties', while the changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Companies' in the initial definition. The contradicted text uses the term 'Parties' to define obligations and agreements. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity as to whether the obligations assigned to 'Parties' also apply to 'Companies'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the contract uses multiple terms (Parties and Companies) interchangeably without clearly defining them as equivalent, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by NaturalNano.
perturbation.changed_text: All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be managed by NaturalNano.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'owned' to 'managed' alters the fundamental right of ownership. Later clauses refer to 'ownership' of intellectual property arising from the JRA. This creates contradiction and introduces ambiguity over who legally owns the non-medical applications developed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, all intellectual property, including but not limited to inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA shall be owned by and assigned to the Parties as follows: o All medical uses and inventions that arise out of this JRA shall be owned by Nanolution. o All purification processes for raw halloysite and all non-medical applications that arise out of this JRA shall be managed by NaturalNano.
perturbation.justification: The original text states NaturalNano 'owned' the purification processes. The changed text modifies this to 'managed'. The contradicted text explicitly states that NaturalNano 'managed' the processes. Since the contract elsewhere refers to 'ownership' of intellectual property, changing 'owned' to 'managed' introduces a contradiction regarding who legally owns the non-medical applications developed, since 'managed' does not imply ownership.

perturbation.original_text: Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Participant, at its discretion, may disclose to another Participant any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Participant, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Participant to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Party' to 'Participant' without redefining the term 'Party' creates confusion. The term Party has been defined as Nanolution and NaturalNano, however the definition of a participant is not listed within the agreement. Subsequent clauses refer to 'Parties' and impose obligations on them. It's unclear if these obligations also apply to 'Participants'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Party' to 'Participant' introduces a contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states obligations for 'Parties' regarding confidential information. Since 'Party' is a defined term (Nanolution and NaturalNano) and 'Participant' is not, it is unclear if 'Participants' are also bound by these obligations. This creates ambiguity and a potential conflict, as obligations binding 'Parties' might not extend to 'Participants', directly contradicting the intention of the agreement to protect confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: All SENSITIVE INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all SENSITIVE INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to 'SENSITIVE INFORMATION' without explicitly defining what constitutes sensitive information, the contract becomes ambiguous. The contract defines 'Confidential Information'. It is not clear if Sensitive Information is the same as Confidential Information. This contradicts the rest of the document, which uses the defined term Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary. No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', which is a defined term within the contract as outlined in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces this with 'SENSITIVE INFORMATION', which is not defined. This creates a contradiction because the contract now uses an undefined term in a section that previously relied on a specific definition. This misaligned terminology introduces ambiguity and contradicts the section that defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.changed_text: The responsibilities of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'obligations' to 'responsibilities' introduces ambiguity as 'Obligations' has a legal standing while 'Responsibility' does not have the same level of legal standing within the agreement. It is not clear whether the two words may be used interchangeably, thereby reducing the enforceable aspects of the term obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; <PAGE> -3- (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'obligations' with 'responsibilities'. The explanation states this introduces ambiguity because 'Obligations' has a stronger legal standing than 'Responsibility', potentially reducing the enforceable aspects of the term. The contradicted text discusses 'written obligation' to maintain confidentiality. If 'responsibilities' replaces 'obligations', it weakens the legal enforceability of the requirement to maintain confidentiality as previously established, creating a contradiction as the 'written obligation' may now be interpreted differently or deemed less binding. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
